title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Execution Trace: Upload Byte Time Count Budget
summary: An upload handler trusts client filenames and content types, accepts unlimited bodies, publishes bytes before scanning, and leaves partial state when the caller disconnects. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upload byte time count budget increment in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Make stable identity, conditional requests, upload records, checksums, and post-failure reconciliation explicit for repeatable operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Upload Byte Time Count Budget · Theory Upload Byte Time Count Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2711 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
